textPlain: An issue with his remote setup is that the remote VPS decrypts packets from the remote laptop, then re-encrypts them for the LAN — this means that the remote VPS can see the plaintext of all those packets.  He’ll need to layer TLS or something similar, or run Wireguard over Wireguard.
I just use Tailscale (headscale)... It's Wireguard in essence but much easier to deploy and maintain.

The refusal to use a control plane tells me the author would probably enjoy openvpn much more than vanilla wireguard.It’s so nice to edit openvpn configuration, maybe some ccd file, restart the openvpn server and see clients reconnect one by one and pick up new options (eg: nee routes etc)

In my opinion Tailscale is the realistic option for most people. The author is familiar with Tailscale having worked with it previously, but my interpretation is that he wanted to get more familiar with the underlying Wireguard technology.
Only if you’re happy with your CPU being pegged at 100% whenever you go beyond 40 mbit/s.I’d rather use wireguard and get 800 mbit/s.
